reasoning: Hamas is under intense pressure from the Trump administration's "Board of Peace" (which set a deadline of approximately April 12, 2026) to disarm and relinquish power in exchange for Gaza's reconstruction following the October 2025 ceasefire. However, Hamas has historically refused to disarm and continues to signal unwillingness to do so unless stringent conditions (complete Israeli withdrawal, etc.) are met. If Hamas is simply defeated militarily without a formal, credible announcement of disarmament from its leadership, the market resolves NO. Given the hardline stance of the group, a voluntary surrender or even a negotiated disarmament under duress remains unlikely (estimated ~10-15% in the near term). The Manifold market was at 16% on April 2. With recent reports of Hamas's rejection of the ultimatum, the probability slightly decreases for the near term. For longer horizons (represented by the 3 requested probabilities), uncertainty increases naturally.
